column4: "description": "Supports 64.0 GT/s operation via PL64G extended capability structures. Provides per-lane equalization control and status for lanes 0–15 enabling Gen6 training. Includes control/status registers for capability enable
column6: and link quality. Designed to work over standard PIPE interface with compatible 64G PHYs. Enables highest bandwidth modes when platform power/PHY allow."
column7: "evidence": "PL64G_* Cap/Control/Status p. 3422–3435; PL64G lane EQ refs p. 1652–1671"
column9: "dependencies": "CX_PL64G_ENABLE && compatible external PHY; proper refclock/PLL; Link training to 64.0 GT/s"
column11: "Remarks": "Availability depends on configuration license and board PHY selection"

column4: "description": "Supports 32.0 GT/s operation through PL32G capability. Exposes margining and link status registers for Gen5 operation. Integrates with Gen5-compliant PHY over PIPE. Allows firmware to verify and tune link health at Gen5. Falls back to lower generations if partner or PHY cannot meet requirements."
column5: "evidence": "PF0_PL32G_CAP and Margining Ext Cap p. 1616–1624 (Exists: CX_PL32G_ENABLE)"
column7: "dependencies": "CX_PL32G_ENABLE; external Gen5 PHY; link partner support"
column9: "Remarks": "Negotiated speed determined by both ends of link"

column4: "description": "Provides 16.0 GT/s operation via PL16G capability registers. Includes parity/error status and link control for Gen4 modes. Compatible with 16G PIPE PHYs and equalization flow. Ensures robust operation with per-lane status and control. Can be combined with margining features for validation."
column7: "dependencies": "External Gen4 PHY; proper refclock; board signal integrity"

column4: "description": "Supports scalable lane widths up to x16 as indicated by lane control/status and margining registers for lanes 0–15. Link training negotiates usable width with partner. Allows bandwidth scaling per system constraints. Per-lane EQ/margining control aids bring-up across widths. Software can read Link Status to confirm negotiated width."
column5: "evidence": "Lane EQ and margining i=0..15 refs p. 3392–3394; 1616–1620; Link Status p. 1395"
column7: "dependencies": "PHY lane availability; board routing; partner device width"
column9: "Remarks": "Lane reversal and polarity handled by PHY/Link training"

column4: "description": "Implements standard PIPE interface to connect to external PCIe PHY. Core supports selecting a custom PHY and provides timing and general PHY configuration options. Facilitates interoperability with various SerDes vendors. Ensures decoupling of controller and PHY implementation. Provides registers for PHY timing adjustments."
column5: "evidence": "Basic Features Config Table 1-2 Phy Type/Phy Selection; Advanced PHY Config/General Options p. 208; PHY Timing p. 212"
column7: "dependencies": "Correct PHY selection in configuration; stable reference clocks; reset sequencing"
column9: "Remarks": "PHY-specific initialization may be done by platform firmware"

column4: "description": "Provides fine-grained Gen3 equalization legality/status registers. Offers control over feedback mode and directed change during training. Exposes coefficient tables and FOM metrics for per-lane tuning. Enables software-assisted EQ optimization when needed. Useful for marginal channels and compliance testing."
column5: "evidence": "GEN3_EQ_COEFF_LEGALITY_STATUS_OFF p. 4068; GEN3_EQ_CONTROL_OFF p. 4070; GEN3_EQ_FB_MODE_DIR_CHANGE_OFF p. 4078; GEN3_FCOEF_* p. 4265–4273"
column7: "dependencies": "Gen3 or higher link operation; debug privilege to access EQ regs"

column4: "description": "Implements PCIe lane margining capability with port-level and per-lane control/status. Allows margin measurements in voltage and timing domains without disturbing traffic. Assists in characterizing link quality at high speeds. Provides control to select lanes and read margin results. Valuable for production test and SI validation."
column7: "dependencies": "Gen4/Gen5/Gen6 capable PHY; link in L0; margining initiated by SW"

column4: "description": "Exposes AXI interfaces to the SoC fabric for memory and message transactions. Provides basic and advanced configuration for IDs
column6: and ordering. Maps PCIe messages to host addresses via dedicated AXI master message registers. Supports tuning of tags and AXI IDs for performance. Ensures flexible integration into varied SoC buses."
column7: "evidence": "Basic AXI Config p. 42; Advanced AXI Config p. 77; AXI_MSTR_MSG_ADDR_LOW/HIGH_OFF p. 2316–2317; 4121–4123; PCIe TAGs and AXI IDs p. 49"
column9: "dependencies": "AXI clock/reset; address map planning; interconnect configuration"
column11: "Remarks": "AXI parameters set at RTL or via CSRs depending on build"

column4: "description": "Provides multiple programmable address translation regions for inbound and outbound PCIe traffic. Uses a viewport mechanism to access region control and base/limit registers. Enables mapping of host CPU addresses to PCIe bus addresses and vice versa. Supports various TLP types and BAR targeting. Essential for enumeration and DMA windows setup."
column5: "evidence": "IATU_VIEWPORT_OFF and region control registers p. 2319–2320 (DSP) / p. 4121–4124 (USP)"
column7: "dependencies": "Controller clock active; iATU regions programmed before use; correct BAR/iATU coherence"

column4: "description": "Optional DMA capability exposed through a Vendor-Specific Extended Capability. Provides base address registers to locate DMA register block and queues. Enables engine-driven memory transfers without CPU intervention. Integrates with PCIe/iATU for addressing. Presence and size depend on core configuration."
column5: "evidence": "PF_VSECDMA_CAPi and VSECDMA_EXT_CAP_HDR_OFF p. 3837; DMA reg map base low/high p. 3845"
column7: "dependencies": "CC_DMA_ENABLE && (CX_MEMORY_MAP_PCIE_TYPE==2 || ==0); iATU windows; memory coherency considerations"
column9: "Remarks": "Requires vendor driver or abstraction in OS"

column4: "description": "Supports classic PCI interrupt signaling via Interrupt Line and Interrupt Pin fields in configuration space. Useful for legacy endpoints or minimal systems. Automatically reported in Type 0/Type 1 header registers. Can be used when MSI/MSI-X are unavailable. Typically lower performance due to shared signaling and ISR latency."
column5: "evidence": "Type0/Type1 headers include INTERRUPT_PIN/LINE p. 1302–1303; VF header fields p. 4727"
column7: "dependencies": "Platform interrupt routing; OS support for INTx"

column4: "description": "Implements Message Signaled Interrupt capability per function. Supports 32/64-bit message addresses and multiple vectors. Configuration via standard MSI capability registers in PF configuration space. Reduces interrupt sharing and improves performance. Widely supported by modern OS kernels."
column5: "evidence": "Device-Wide MSI Capability p. 85; Per-Function MSI Register Configuration p. 143"
column7: "dependencies": "OS enables MSI during enumeration; proper MSI target address programmed"

column4: "description": "Provides MSI-X with large vector tables and per-vector masking. Table and PBA are located in memory mapped by BARs. Offers finer-grained interrupt distribution and affinity. Improves scalability for multi-queue devices. Fully standardized capability structure."
column5: "evidence": "MSI-X Capability p. 144; MSI-X PBA Offset and BIR Register p. 1481"
column7: "dependencies": "BAR programmed to map MSI-X Table/PBA; OS MSI-X support"
column9: "Remarks": "Ideal for high-throughput devices with multiple queues"

column4: "description": "Supports PCIe Lightweight Notification to signal events with minimal overhead. Configured per function with device-wide options. Can complement or reduce reliance on MSI for certain flows. Useful for power-optimized notification paths. Requires coordination with OS or firmware policy."
column5: "evidence": "Lightweight Notification Support Options p. 108; Per-function LN configuration p. 154"
column7: "dependencies": "LN enabled in core build and per PF; consumer software support"

column4: "description": "Provides readiness indicators allowing software to determine when a function is operational. Device-wide options control support and semantics. Helps sequence initialization across PFs and VFs. Reduces race conditions at bring-up. Complements link and power state checks."
column5: "evidence": "Readiness Support Options p. 105; PF0 Readiness Configuration p. 155"
column7: "dependencies": "Per-function config; driver polling or interrupt scheme"

column4: "description": "Implements Active State Power Management controls for L0s and L1 states. Provides registers to set policy and acknowledge frequency. Works with Link Control to enable/disable states per policy. Facilitates dynamic power reduction under light traffic. Requires coordination with OS ASPM policy."
column7: "dependencies": "Platform ASPM policy enabled; stable clocking; peer support"

column4: "description": "Supports PCIe L1 Substates for deeper idle power savings including L1.1
column5: L1.2 and Common Mode Restore (CPM). Exposes capability and control registers to negotiate and manage substates. Reduces PHY and logic power while maintaining quick resume. Availability depends on core build flags. Complements ASPM and LTR for fine-grained power control."
column6: "evidence": "L1SUB_CAP_HEADER_REG p. 3543; L1SUB_CAPABILITY_REG p. 3545; L1SUB_CONTROL1_REG p. 3549 (Exists: CX_L1_SUBSTATES_ENABLE && CX_MEMORY_MAP_PCIE_TYPE<2)"
column7: "type": "Explicit"

column4: "description": "Implements LTR extended capability to communicate acceptable service latencies to the platform. Software can program latency thresholds per function. Enables platform power managers to make informed clock/power gating decisions. Essential when using L1 Substates and ASPM. Improves power without sacrificing QoS."
column7: "dependencies": "OS/firmware LTR policy; function-specific service models"

column4: "description": "Exposes standard Power Budgeting extended capability. Allows reporting and management of device power consumption classes. Provides data select/control and budget data registers. Useful in platform-level power orchestration. Not performance-critical but aids system management."
column7: "dependencies": "Platform software that queries PCIe power budgeting"

column4: "description": "Provides NPEM extended capability for enclosure and device management. Offers capability
column6: and status registers for management functions. Can be used to coordinate power and thermal states in managed enclosures. Useful in server backplanes and storage. Availability conditioned by core configuration."
column7: "evidence": "NPEM_EXT_CAP_HDR_OFF / NPEM_CAP/CTRL/STATUS p. 3847–3862"

column4: "description": "Supports device-wide options to reduce power under emergency conditions. Allows firmware or management agents to trigger power reduction behaviors. Complements standard PCIe power management features. Intended for thermal or power budget excursions. Implementation specifics controlled by configuration parameters."
column7: "dependencies": "Platform policy and sensors; management firmware hooks"

column4: "description": "Implements PCIe AER extended capability for advanced error logging and reporting. Provides correctable/uncorrectable error status
column6: and severity controls. Enables error injection and reporting to software for diagnostics. Helps isolate link and protocol issues. Presence gated by build-time option."
column7: "evidence": "AER_EXT_CAP_HDR_OFF p. 1488 (Exists: AER_ENABLE && CX_MEMORY_MAP_PCIE_TYPE<2)"
column9: "dependencies": "AER_ENABLE; OS AER support; proper interrupt routing"

column4: "description": "Provides lane-level error status registers to detect and localize physical layer errors. Exposes counters and flags per lane. Aids in diagnosing SI issues and marginal channels. Can be monitored during stress testing. Complements AER for detailed link insight."
column7: "dependencies": "Link active; access to SPCIE registers"

column4: "description": "Tracks local data parity mismatches at the PHY interface including retimer paths. Provides status registers for quick identification of parity faults. Helps validate PHY and retimer integrity at high speeds. Useful during production test. Not typically polled in normal operation."
column7: "dependencies": "Gen4+ operation; PHY exposes parity status via controller"

column4: "description": "Registers exist to generate or monitor vendor-specific DLLPs for testing. Facilitates protocol-level experimentation and validation. Not used in normal production traffic. Accessible under privileged modes. Complements other debug registers."
column7: "dependencies": "Lab/test environment; link in L0"

column4: "description": "Allows software to force certain link behaviors for bring-up. Provides controls to influence the LTSSM and link settings. Enables controlled retraining
column6: or disable/enable transitions. Useful to recover from error states during validation. Should not be used during normal runtime."
column7: "evidence": "PORT_FORCE_OFF p. 2169; PORT_LINK_CTRL_OFF p. 2176; ACK_F_ASPM_CTRL_OFF p. 2172"

column4: "description": "Implements Secondary PCIe extended capability including Link Control 3. Provides lane error status and additional link management registers. Extends standard PCIe capabilities for advanced link control. Supports per-lane equalization programming. Aids high-speed link robustness."
column5: "evidence": "SPCIE Link Control 3 and Lane Error Status p. 3357–3363 (USP) / p. 1587–1592 (DSP)"
column7: "dependencies": "SPCIE enabled in build; access via extended cap list"
column9: "Remarks": "Mainly for diagnostics and speed control extensions"

column4: "description": "Supports ARI to extend function numbering and improve downstream port scalability. Provides ARI capability registers per function. Enables more virtual functions per physical device when combined with SR-IOV. Helps reduce bus numbers and improve enumeration efficiency. Standardized extended capability."
column7: "dependencies": "Downstream ports and OS must support ARI; SR-IOV for max benefit"

column4: "description": "Provides ATS extended capability allowing functions to request translations from an IOMMU. Reduces address translation overhead for DMA. Improves performance in virtualized systems. Requires OS/IOMMU cooperation. Configurable per function."
column5: "evidence": "Per-Function ATS Register Configuration p. 149; SII ATS signals p. 1201"
column7: "dependencies": "IOMMU with ATS support; OS enabling; proper PASID/PRI stack if used"

column4: "description": "Implements ACS to control peer-to-peer transactions and redirect to upstream. Enforces isolation between functions for security and virtualization. Provides ACS capability and control bits per function. Often required for PCIe device assignment. Works with IOMMU policies."
column7: "dependencies": "Platform ACS policy; OS virtualization stack"

column4: "description": "Supports Process Address Space ID via TLP Prefix for address space tagging. Enables fine-grained context tracking for DMA. Useful in virtualization and shared device models. Advertised via capability registers. Often combined with ATS/PRI for best results."
column5: "evidence": "TLP Prefix Support / PASID Capability Register Defaults p. 127–129"
column7: "dependencies": "OS/IOMMU support; PASID allocation; function configuration"
column9: "Remarks": "Required by some CXL use cases as well"

column4: "description": "Provides TPH extended capability allowing devices to include processing hints with TLPs. Can reduce latency by steering requests to optimal caches/cores. Controlled per PF with standard registers. Benefits depend on platform support. Not required for functional correctness."
column7: "dependencies": "Platform/OS honoring TPH; device driver enablement"

column4: "description": "Supports Single Root I/O Virtualization with multiple Virtual Functions per Physical Function. Provides PF registers to configure VF count
column6: and capabilities. Exposes VF extended capabilities and power management pointers. Enables efficient device sharing among guests. Widely used in data center devices."
column7: "evidence": "Device-Wide SR-IOV features p. 112–114; PF0 SR-IOV Register Configuration p. 185; VF BARs p. 188; VF PM cap pointer p. 4726"
column9: "dependencies": "OS SR-IOV support; IOMMU; ARI for large VF counts"

column4: "description": "Implements standard PCI Type 0 (endpoint) and Type 1 (bridge) configuration headers. Provides per-function BAR setup and defaults for address decoding. Enables standard enumeration by BIOS/OS. Includes device/vendor IDs and command/status registers. Required for PCIe compliance."
column5: "evidence": "PF_TYPE0_HDRi regs (DEVICE_ID_VENDOR_ID_REG) p. 3074; TYPE1_DEV_ID_VEND_ID_REG p. 1302; TYPE1_STATUS_COMMAND_REG p. 1303; BAR Setup For PF0 p. 165"
column7: "dependencies": "Enumeration by firmware/OS; iATU programmed accordingly"

column4: "description": "Provides VPD capability to expose device-specific information in a standardized format. Includes base and data registers for VPD access. Useful for serviceability and inventory. Accessed via PCI config space. Optional and may be read-only."
column7: "dependencies": "VPD storage programmed or fused; OS tools"

column4: "description": "Exposes DesignWare DBI/DBI2 interface to shadow/override parts of PCIe configuration space. Allows privileged firmware to adjust capability registers safely. Useful for late binding of configuration parameters. Facilitates testing and OEM customization. Access is restricted to prevent OS conflicts."
column5: "evidence": "DBI2 Shadow Block: PF PCI Express Capability Structure p. 3234"
column7: "dependencies": "Firmware access only; coordination with OS enumeration"

column4: "description": "Provides readable registers indicating controller IP version number and type. Helps software adapt to feature sets and errata workarounds. Useful in field diagnostics for determining core revisions. Read-only information aids logging. Present in both DSP and USP maps."
column7: "dependencies": "None"

column4: "description": "Provides Compute Express Link related capabilities when enabled
column5: including RCRB extended capability and DVSEC structures. Supports RCiEP transport and FlexBus control/status. Adds CXL-specific power phases and test registers. Integrates CXS interface error/status reporting. Only present when the CXL option is configured."
column6: "evidence": "CXL_RCRB_PCIE_EXT_CAP_HDR_OFF p. 3878; CXL RCiEP DVSEC p. 3920; CXL_GPF_DEV_DVSEC_PHASE_2_ACT_POWER_OFF p. 3918; CXS_ERROR_STAT_OFF p. 3834; Device Test DVSEC p. 3955–3959"
column7: "type": "Explicit"
column8: "dependencies": "CX_CXL_ENABLE; platform CXL PHY; BIOS/OS CXL support"

column4: "description": "Includes DVSEC registers for CCIX transport over PCIe when enabled. Enables coherent accelerator interconnect features. Provides capability discovery and control via extended capabilities. Not used in pure PCIe deployments. Presence depends on configuration."
column5: "evidence": "Device-Wide PCIe Features/Capabilities Config – CCIX Transport DVSEC p. 134"
column7: "dependencies": "Build-time enable; OS/driver stack for CCIX"
column9: "Remarks": "Mutually exclusive with some modes depending on config"

column4: "description": "Provides configuration options for FLIT mode operation associated with higher PCIe generations. Adjusts internal transport to flit-based encoding for efficiency. May impact buffering and latency characteristics. Intended for Gen5/Gen6 usage. Controlled via device-wide options."
column7: "dependencies": "High-speed link negotiated; compatible PHY; build-time enable"
column9: "Remarks": "Primarily internal but may affect performance tuning"

column4: "description": "Exposes silicon debug registers to control equalization settings beyond standard mechanisms. Provides multiple control pages for detailed adjustments. Aids in characterization of channels and PHY behavior. Not intended for normal runtime. Requires privileged lab access."
column7: "dependencies": "Debug build access; stable link"

column4: "description": "Vendor-Specific Extended Capability provides event counting and time-based analysis. Enables software to profile link and transaction behaviors. Useful for performance tuning and workload characterization. Control/data registers expose metrics and time stamps. Complements external analyzers with on-chip telemetry."
column5: "evidence": "VENDOR_SPECIFIC_HEADER_REG p. 1782/3607; Event Counter Control/Data p. 1784/1787 (DSP) and 3609/3613 (USP); Time-based Analysis p. 1788/1791 (DSP)"
column7: "dependencies": "VSEC enabled; driver to collect/report metrics"

column4: "description": "Software can influence or retrigger link training using port force and link control registers. Assists in recovering from error states or testing transitions. Enables speed changes and disable/enable sequences under control. Should be used carefully to avoid disrupting traffic. Not a routine operational feature."
column7: "dependencies": "Administrative privilege; quiesced traffic"
column9: "Remarks": "Use in lab scripts and bring-up tools"

column4: "description": "Provides configuration to map PCIe request tags to AXI IDs and control outstanding transactions. Enables optimization of interconnect utilization and ordering. Tuning can reduce head-of-line blocking. Impacts DMA and CPU-initiated traffic performance. Requires system-level analysis to set appropriately."
column5: "evidence": "PCIe TAGs and AXI IDs p. 49; Basic/Advanced AXI Config p. 42/77"
column7: "dependencies": "AXI interconnect limits; device queue depths"

column4: Dual-Mode
column7: "description": "Controller can be configured as Endpoint (EP)
column10: or Switch Port (upstream/downstream). Enables reuse across host and device roles. Role selection is parameterized or pin-selectable depending on build. Per-role configuration spaces and headers are provided. Facilitates broad deployment scenarios."
column16: "dependencies": "Build-time device type and board strapping; firmware enumeration model"
column18: "Remarks": "This feature list targets host DM/RP usage"

column2: "feature_name": "MSI-X Table and PBA Structures in Memory"
column4: "description": "MSI-X uses in-memory Table and Pending Bit Array structures referenced by BARs. Allows large vector sets and per-vector masking. Software programs BIR and offsets to establish locations. Improves interrupt scalability and CPU affinity control. Standard discovery via capability list."
column5: "evidence": "MSI-X PBA Offset and BIR Register p. 1481; MSI-X Capability p. 144"
column7: "dependencies": "Memory BAR sizing; cacheability attributes; OS support"

column4: "description": "At Gen6 speeds
column5: provides per-lane equalization controls allowing fine adjustments. Exposes control/status for each lane 0–15. Helps tailor settings to channel variations across the bundle. Can improve margin and BER on marginal lanes. Typically used during validation and tuned automatically by training otherwise."
column6: "evidence": "PL64G lane EQ control/status regs p. 3422–3435 and 1652–1671"
column7: "type": "Explicit"
column8: "dependencies": "Gen6 capable PHY; link training phases executed"
column10: "Remarks": "Leave at defaults unless SI requires tuning"

column4: "description": "Viewport mechanism allows indirect access to multiple inbound/outbound ATU regions using a small CSR window. Reduces CSR footprint while scaling number of regions. Software selects a region via viewport and then programs control/base/limit. Essential for complex address maps. Used by standard DW PCIe drivers."
column7: "dependencies": "Clocked CSR access; proper sequencing during init"

column4: "description": "Standard PCI Express Capability structure is implemented per function. Provides Device Capabilities
column7: and Link Control/Status. Software uses these to enable features like Max Payload
column9: and link power states. Ensures compliance and interoperability. Header pointers link to extended capability space for more features."
column10: "evidence": "DEVICE_CAPABILITIES_REG p. 1373; LINK_CAPABILITIES_REG p. 1388; LINK_CONTROL_LINK_STATUS_REG p. 1395; LINK_CAPABILITIES2_REG p. 3215; LINK_CONTROL2_LINK_STATUS2_REG p. 3221"
